name: Crafting Unlocks
description: A progression rule that makes recipes, stations, material tiers, or crafting categories available over time.
design_notes: Crafting unlocks control when players can turn resources into power. They need clear unlock_requirement_id values, recipe_id grants, prerequisite rules, and migration behavior so old saves do not lose recipes and players know what activity opens the next tier.
design_purpose: Pace crafting complexity so players learn resources and recipes in manageable groups.
Reward exploration, quests, skill investment, or milestones with new production options.
Prevent late-game recipes from bypassing intended combat, economy, or survival progression.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are learning better techniques and expanding what their gathered materials can become.

parameters.name: recipe_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the recipe unlocked by the progression rule.

parameters.name: unlock_requirement_id
parameters.description: Requirement that must be satisfied before the recipe becomes craftable.

parameters.name: station_tier_required
parameters.description: Minimum crafting station tier or facility level required to use the unlocked recipe.

parameters.name: material_tier_gate
parameters.description: Material tier used to group related recipes and communicate progression stage.

parameters.name: retroactive_unlock_flag
parameters.description: Whether players who already met the requirement receive the recipe automatically after patches.

edge_cases: A recipe is unlocked but the required station tier is missing or destroyed.
A patch moves a recipe to a new unlock_requirement_id while old saves already know it.
Multiplayer crafting stations need to decide whether unlocks belong to the account, character, party, or world.
A recipe is visible before materials exist, making the unlock feel like a bug instead of progress.
common_bugs: Crafting UI checks recipe visibility while the transaction validator checks a different unlock table.
Blueprint pickups grant duplicate recipes because recipe_id completion is not idempotent.
Retroactive unlock scans run every login and repeatedly display the same recipe notifications.
Station tier changes invalidate recipe filters but the UI cache is not refreshed.
balancing_notes: Unlock recipe groups in clusters that create new choices, not just one strictly superior replacement at a time.
Late recipes should account for material drop rates and station costs so unlocking does not immediately bottleneck elsewhere.
If recipes are missable, provide alternate unlock routes or clear warnings before permanent lockout.
accessibility_notes: Show locked recipes with readable requirement text when spoilers are acceptable.
Provide sorting and filtering by station, material tier, and unlock source for large recipe lists.

implementation_notes.unity: Store recipe_id and unlock_requirement_id in ScriptableObjects or imported tables; crafting transactions should query the same unlock service used by the UI before consuming materials.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent recipes and unlock rules as Resources and keep known_recipe_ids in progression_state; migration code should add retroactive recipe IDs without duplicating notifications.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use DataAssets or DataTables for recipes and a progression subsystem for unlock validation; multiplayer worlds should define whether recipe grants live on PlayerState, SaveGame, or world state.
implementation_notes.web: Keep recipe definitions in JSON and known_recipe_ids in save data; run unlock checks as pure functions so UI filtering and craft commits cannot disagree.

example_games.title: Subnautica
example_games.note: Uses discovered blueprints to unlock crafting recipes.

example_games.title: Terraria
example_games.note: Progressively exposes crafting options through materials and stations.
